system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

nu              1e-05;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-07;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    epsilon
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    epsilonFinal
    {
        $epsilon;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    4;
    nCorrectors         5;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        U               0.3;
        p               0.1;
    }
}

residualControl
{
    U
    {
        relTol      0.1;
        tolerance   1e-05;
    }
    p
    {
        relTol      0.1;
        tolerance   1e-05;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The simulation is running but **stuck in an infinite PIMPLE outer loop** — it's performing iterations 1, 2, 3... within a single time step without ever exiting to advance to the next time step. Looking at the current log:

1. **Iteration 1**: Residuals drop from ~0.001 to ~0.00005 (good convergence)
2. **Iteration 2**: Residuals drop to ~1e-05 level (excellent convergence)
3. **Iteration 3**: Residuals are at machine-epsilon levels (`Ux: 5.73e-06`, `Uy: 4.01e-05`)
4. Yet **no "End" marker** appears — the solver never leaves the PIMPLE loop

The problem is the **residual control logic**. As diagnosed in Attempt 8, `relTol 0` means the solver requires residuals to reach exactly `1e-05` to exit the outer loop. But `Uy` is at `4.01e-05` (which is > `1e-05`), so the exit condition is never met. Previous attempts tried `relTol 0.1` but that was rejected or didn't stick.

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue from Attempt 8 persists: with `relTol 0`, the PIMPLE outer loop never satisfies the convergence criterion because `Uy` residual (`4.01e-05`) exceeds the `tolerance` of `1e-05`. Simply changing `relTol` to `0.1` should work, but the system may have reset. The deeper issue is that the **outer loop exit strategy** needs to be handled differently.

Looking at the pitzDaily tutorial reference more carefully, the standard approach is to use `residualControl` with `relTol` set to a reasonable value (like `0.1`) AND to ensure the `nOuterCorrectors` is set appropriately. The key insight from successful pitzDaily runs is that the residual control should allow exit when residuals are sufficiently small relative to their initial values.

## Proposed Solutions

### 1. Fix `residualControl` with proper `relTol` and `tolerance`

In `system/fvSolution`, update the `residualControl` block to use `relTol 0.1` (meaning exit when residuals are within 10% of their initial value):

```
residualControl
{
    U
    {
        relTol      0.1;
        tolerance   1e-05;
    }
    p
    {
        relTol      0.1;
        tolerance   1e-05;
    }
}
```

This is the same as Attempt 8 but must be applied **permanently**. The `relTol 0` was the culprit — it demanded exact convergence to `1e-05` which `Uy` never achieved.

### 2. Increase `nOuterCorrectors` as a safety fallback

Increase `nOuterCorrectors` from `4` to `6` in the `PIMPLE` sub-dictionary. This provides a hard ceiling so even if residual control never triggers, the outer loop will eventually exit after 6 iterations and advance the time step.

### 3. Ensure `nCorrectors` is reasonable

Keep `nCorrectors 5` — this governs the inner correction sweeps per outer iteration and is fine as-is.

### Summary

The **single definitive fix** is to set `relTol 0.1` in the `residualControl` sub-dictionary for both `U` and `p`. The simulation has proven it converges well within each time step (residuals drop below `1e-05` quickly), but the `relTol 0` prevents the outer loop from recognizing this convergence. With `relTol 0.1`, the outer loop will exit after the residuals are within 10% of their initial values, allowing proper time advancement to `endTime 0.3`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSolution', 'changes': 'Change relTol from 0.1 to 0.01 for U entry in residualControl block; Change relTol from 0.1 to 0.01 for p entry in residualControl block'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-05.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
